heroHeadline: Russia's Victory Day Parade to March Without Heavy Military Equipment — Signal of Armored Materiel Depletion
heroSubtitle: Russian drones struck Odesa's residential buildings and a hotel overnight into April 27, injuring 11 people including 2 children. A simultaneous attack on Chornomorsk port (30 km southwest of Odesa) hit a storage tank, triggering a massive sunflower oil spill of approximately 6,000 tons into the Black Sea — Ukraine's worst wartime maritime environmental disaster. Ukraine and Norway announced joint production of 'mid-strike' drones — thousands of units, fully Norway-financed — marking a major shift toward European co-manufacturing of Ukrainian weapons. President Zelensky submitted proposals to extend martial law and mobilization for 90 more days until August 2, 2026; separately, Ukraine dispatched a delegation to the NPT Review Conference in New York (April 27–May 22). Russia suffered 810 additional personnel losses (cumulative ~1,326,460 since Feb 24, 2022), with active fighting continuing across Lyman, Pokrovsk, Kupyansk, and Huliaipole sectors.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OHCHR, ISW, CSIS, Ukrainian MoD, US DoD, NATO, IAEA, EU Council, UK MoD, and major news outlets including Reuters, BBC, AP, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Kyiv Independent, Euronews, and RBC-Ukraine. Casualty figures are estimates; Russia has never released official figures.
